metadata.title: Skill Guardrails Control Catalog (SG)
metadata.remarks: Controls for governing Agent Skills (SKILL.md directories) in DeepAgents/LangGraph runtimes. Prop namespace: https://sw30labs.com/ns/osg

params.label: severity at or above which human approval is required

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Every skill directory is scanned by an approved scanner before it is exposed to any agent. Findings are normalized to a severity scale and compared to the declared risk appetite (max severity, minimum score, minimum grade). Skills exceeding the appetite are denied admission; the scanner failing, timing out, or returning no score when a score threshold is set is treated as a denial (fail closed).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A content digest (SHA-256 over all files) is computed at admission and re-verified before skill content is loaded at runtime, preventing time-of-check to time-of-use substitution of scanned skill content.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Each agent, subagent, and role is granted visibility only to explicitly allowed skills (glob patterns). Deny patterns take precedence over allow patterns. Absent an allow list, no skills are exposed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Tool calls are pre-filtered per agent and enforced at runtime by middleware. Skills may further restrict which tools are usable in their context. Deny patterns take precedence; unknown tools outside an allow list are denied.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Curated skill directories are read-only to agents; mutations to personal skill areas require human approval. Rules are materialized as DeepAgents FilesystemPermission objects.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Skills whose scan severity meets the interrupt threshold, or which carry an explicit interrupt effect, require human approval before exposure. Approval decisions are recorded.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Skills must declare required metadata (e.g. owner) in SKILL.md frontmatter; skills missing required provenance metadata are denied admission.

params.label: require an LLM rubric-judge evidence stream (true|false)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to static scanning, skills are reviewed by an independent LLM judge against a fixed rubric (intent alignment, scope minimality, tool justification, data boundary, influence boundary, provenance, human oversight). Failed criteria produce findings at rubric-defined severities that flow through the same admission gates as static findings. Where required, absence of the rubric evidence stream is itself a denial (fail closed). The judge treats skill content strictly as untrusted data; judge failure is a critical finding.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Every admission and tool-call decision (effect, reason, matched rule, skill digest) is emitted to an audit sink, suitable for serialization as OSCAL assessment-results.
